name: Tuna Melt
tagline: Tuna salad sandwich pressed under cheddar until golden and bubbling
highlight: Tuna, cheese, butter, bread — diner-counter perfect

hero.alt: Tuna melt with golden crust and bubbling cheddar
hero.caption: tuna melt — diner classic

healthFacts: Canned tuna packed in olive oil retains more omega-3s than water-packed.
Adding lemon zest brightens canned tuna without extra sodium.

steps.title: Mix the tuna salad
steps.detail: Drain tuna and break up with a fork. Stir in mayo, celery, onion, lemon zest and juice, salt, pepper. Taste — it should pop.
steps.tip: A teaspoon of Dijon adds depth without changing the character.

steps.title: Build the sandwich
steps.detail: Lay cheddar on one slice, pile tuna salad, top with another slice of cheddar, then bread. Cheese on both sides locks the filling in.
steps.tip: Cheese acts as a moisture barrier — keeps the bread from going soggy.

steps.title: Butter and grill low
steps.detail: Butter the outsides. Cook in a skillet over medium-low 4 minutes per side, lid on, until golden and the cheese is fully melted.
steps.tip: Lid traps heat to melt the inside before the outside burns.

steps.title: Rest and slice
steps.detail: Rest 30 seconds, slice diagonally, and eat immediately.
steps.tip: A few cornichons on the side cut the richness.
